paper_title: Effects of marine sediment as agricultural substrate on soil microbial diversity: an amplicon sequencing study

methods.description: This method involves using R software to perform statistical analysis on biodiversity data, including alpha and beta diversity comparisons, and testing the significance of community structure groups.

steps.description: Alpha diversity comparisons were performed using Kruskal-Wallis non-parametric test.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Kruskal-Wallis non-parametric test for alpha diversity

steps.description: Beta diversity distance matrices were used to calculate principal coordinates analysis (PCoA) and to make ordination plots.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate PCoA and create ordination plots from beta diversity matrices

steps.description: The significance of groups present in community structure was tested using Permanova and ANOSIM tests.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Permanova and ANOSIM tests for community structure significance

steps.description: A multivariate analogue of Levene's test for homogeneity of variances, Permdisp test, was used to identify location vs. dispersion effects.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Permdisp test for homogeneity of variances

steps.description: Differential relative abundance of taxa was tested using ANCOM and Kruskal Wallis non-parametric test.
steps.searchable_summary: test differential relative abundance using ANCOM and Kruskal Wallis

steps.description: After Kruskal Wallis, Conover's test with FDR Benjamini-Hochberg correction was added for pairwise comparison.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Conover's test with FDR Benjamini-Hochberg correction for pairwise comparison

methods.description: This method involves processing raw sequencing reads using QIIME2 and Dada2 for quality control, subsampling, and calculating diversity metrics.

steps.description: Raw demultiplexed forward and reverse reads were processed with microbiome bioinformatics platform QIIME2.
Dada2 was used as QIIME 2 plugin for sequence quality control.
steps.searchable_summary: perform sequence quality control using Dada2

steps.description: All the samples were subsampled up to 24,665 reads to even sample size and make quantitative comparisons.
steps.searchable_summary: subsample reads to 24,665 for even sample size

steps.description: Phylotype data was used to calculate alpha diversity metrics: Observed OTUs, Evenness, and Shannon's diversity index.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate alpha diversity metrics from phylotype data

steps.description: Phylotype and phylogenetic data were used to calculate beta diversity metrics: Unweighted Unifrac, Weighted Unifrac, Jaccard, and Bray Curtis distances.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate beta diversity metrics from phylotype and phylogenetic data

steps.description: Taxonomic assignment of phylotypes was performed using a Bayesian Classifier trained with Silva database version 138.
steps.searchable_summary: perform taxonomic assignment using Bayesian Classifier with Silva database

methods.method_name: Analysis Based on Clusters of Orthologous Genes (COGs)
methods.description: This method involves analyzing protein sequences using the COG database to infer functions and classify them based on their representation in different samples.

steps.description: Analyzing the results with the COG database, 4291 protein sequences with correspondence to a COG number were identified in the bacterial genome of the control samples.
steps.searchable_summary: identify protein sequences with COG numbers in control samples

steps.description: For samples containing marine port sediment, the number of correlated sequences was slightly higher.
steps.searchable_summary: compare COG-identified sequences in marine sediment samples

steps.description: By inferring the function of the identified proteins and classifying them, the results showed the most represented functional groups.
steps.searchable_summary: infer and classify protein functions from COG data

methods.method_name: Analysis Based on the Kyoto Encyclopedia of Genes and Genomes (KEGG)
methods.description: This method involves identifying metabolic pathways in soil samples using taxonomic profiling and comparing results to the KEGG database.

steps.description: Taxonomic profiling was performed on soil samples and the results were compared to the KEGG database.
steps.searchable_summary: perform taxonomic profiling and compare to KEGG database

steps.description: Enrichment analysis was performed on the significant pathways using Fisher's test.
steps.searchable_summary: perform enrichment analysis using Fisher's test on significant pathways

steps.description: Based on this analysis, 61 pathways were defined as more enriched, and among those, 30 pathways were identified as dominant.
steps.searchable_summary: identify enriched and dominant pathways from analysis
